name: Darul Huda Corporation
mission: Darul Huda Islamic Center is not just a mosque for prayers rather it is a center for preserving an Islamic identity. Helping to build and support a diverse and friendly Muslim community represented by dozens of different ethnicities based on the Holy Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).
programs: Quran Recitation and Islamic Studies Classes

dimension_explanations.alignment: Darul Huda shows medium alignment for Muslim donors as a zakat-eligible entity serving religious and cultural needs [3]. The urgency is categorized as religious-cultural, though the alignment score is tempered by limited public documentation regarding its historical track record [2].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates a capacity-building model with a cost per beneficiary of approximately $234.98 [1]. While financial health is strong with a 97.5% program expense ratio, the overall impact score is moderate because evidence of long-term effectiveness relies primarily on self-reported data [4].

strengths.detail: The organization directs <cite id="1">97.5% of its expenses to programs</cite>, significantly exceeding the <cite id="4">peer program ratio median of 85.3%</cite>.
Darul Huda maintains <cite id="1">14.7 months of working capital</cite>, ensuring long-term sustainability for its <cite id="2">religious and educational infrastructure</cite>.
The charity spends <cite id="1">$0.00 to raise every $1</cite>, allowing all contributions to support <cite id="2">community services and zakat distribution</cite>.

strengths_deep_dive: Darul Huda Corp exhibits a highly efficient financial profile characterized by a <cite id="1">97.5% program expense ratio</cite>, which is rare even among high-performing religious organizations. This efficiency is supported by a <cite id="1">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, suggesting that the organization relies on organic community support rather than paid solicitations. Furthermore, the organization has managed a <cite id="4">33.0% revenue growth rate</cite> over the last three years, reaching <cite id="4">$975,808 in total revenue</cite> while maintaining <cite id="1">97.5% independent board oversight</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: Darul Huda Corp, operating as the Darul Huda Islamic Center, serves as a vital hub for <cite id="2">religious, cultural, and educational opportunities</cite> within the Springfield, Virginia community. The organization has demonstrated significant financial growth, reporting <cite id="4">total revenue of $975,808</cite> in the 2023 tax year, representing a <cite id="4">33.0% three-year revenue CAGR</cite>. A defining characteristic of the charity is its lean operational model; it maintains a <cite id="1">program expense ratio of 97.5%</cite>, ensuring that every dollar of public support is directed toward its core community services. These services include <cite id="2">Quran recitation and Islamic studies classes</cite>, as well as large-scale community events such as <cite id="2">Ramadan Iftar dinners and Eid festivals</cite>. While the organization operates with high financial efficiency, its current impact measurement is primarily focused on service delivery rather than long-term outcome tracking. The charity maintains <cite id="1">strong liquidity with 14.7 months of working capital</cite>, providing a stable foundation for its ongoing <cite id="2">zakat and sadaqah distribution</cite> programs.
